_comment: In-scope known vulnerabilities per target, limited to the vuln classes PentraAI's modules actually test (idor/bola, broken_auth/jwt, ssrf, bfla, race_condition). This is the DENOMINATOR for detection rate. Do NOT include out-of-scope classes (SQLi, XSS, etc.) here — PentraAI is not designed to find them, so counting them would unfairly deflate the score. Sources: OWASP crAPI challenges page, OWASP Juice Shop companion guide.

in_scope_vulns.title: BOLA: access another user's vehicle location
in_scope_vulns.notes: Vehicle IDs are GUIDs harvested from the Community page.

in_scope_vulns.title: BOLA: access another user's mechanic reports
JWT forgery (alg=none / weak secret / alg confusion)
in_scope_vulns.notes: crAPI accepts forged JWTs for full access.

in_scope_vulns.title: BFLA: delete another user's video via admin endpoint
Race: redeem an already-claimed coupon (candidate)
in_scope_vulns.notes: OPTIONAL/CANDIDATE — verify this is actually race-exploitable in your build before counting it.

in_scope_vulns.title: IDOR: view another user's basket
in_scope_vulns.notes: Returns basket by numeric id with no ownership check.

in_scope_vulns.title: IDOR: add item to another user's basket
IDOR: forged feedback via client UserId
in_scope_vulns.notes: UserId trusted from client — may be outside current module coverage.

in_scope_vulns.title: JWT: forge RSA-signed token (Tier 2 / alg confusion)
SSRF: request a hidden internal resource
